channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police arrest protesters during Victoria Park Olympic stadium site stand-off in Brisbane
item.description: Police and Brisbane City Council officers have moved to dismantle First Nations protest camps at Victoria Park in Brisbane, resulting in at least two arrests during a tense stand-off. The camps were e...

item.title: Argentina names 26-man World Cup squad with Lionel Messi as captain
item.description: Argentina has officially revealed its final 26-player squad for the FIFA World Cup, with Lionel Messi once again taking the lead role as captain and key figure of the team. The announcement confirms a...

item.title: Tshwane issues emergency water response as Rand Water maintenance threatens widespread supply disruptions
item.description: The City of Tshwane has activated an emergency response plan ahead of a major planned maintenance shutdown by Rand Water and Eskom, which is expected to significantly affect water supply across parts ...

item.title: Fame Academy Contestants: Life After the Show and Career Highlights
item.description: Fame Academy, a British talent show from over two decades ago, launched the careers of several notable singers, though its run was brief due to low ratings. David Sneddon, winner of the first series, ...

item.title: Analysis of Australia’s Rich List: wealth trends, property dominance and billionaire comparisons
item.description: This article examines the structure and trends behind Australia’s Rich List using a data-driven approach presented through seven charts. It focuses on how wealth is distributed among Australia’s riche...

item.title: Trump expresses indifference to midterms while focusing on Iran, raising concerns among Republicans
item.description: President Donald Trump made controversial remarks during a recent White House cabinet meeting, stating that he 'doesn't care about the midterms' and is instead prioritizing preventing Iran from develo...

item.title: JSC opens applications for 33 tribunal and senior judiciary-related positions
item.description: The Judicial Service Commission (JSC) of Kenya has advertised 33 job vacancies targeting qualified Kenyan professionals for both middle and senior-level roles within the Judiciary and associated tribu...

item.title: Ben Foster Suggests Manchester United Target Lewandowski on One-Year Deal Amid Multiple Transfer Plans
item.description: Former Manchester United goalkeeper Ben Foster has recommended that the club pursue Robert Lewandowski in the upcoming summer transfer window, suggesting a one-year contract similar to past signings l...

item.title: Hundreds of Bluesky accounts compromised in alleged pro-Russian influence operation
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a coordinated cyber influence operation in which hundreds of accounts on the social media platform Bluesky were reportedly hijacked and used to spread pro-Russian propaganda...

item.title: Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy Address Competition for Vikings Starting Quarterback Role
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings’ quarterback competition became a major topic during offseason workouts after Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy gave noticeably different answers when discussing their relationship ...

item.title: Cholistan water crisis pushes seasonal migration as ponds dry up
item.description: The Cholistan Desert in Bahawalpur is facing an acute water shortage as about one-third of the region’s 2,000 open water ponds, locally called tobas, have dried up due to extreme heat and high evapora...

item.title: Paul McCartney reflects on memories, The Beatles, and Oasis influences behind his new nostalgic album
item.description: Paul McCartney, aged 83, has discussed the inspirations behind his 18th solo album, The Boys of Dungeon Lane, in an in-depth interview reflecting on memory, collaboration, and musical influence. The r...

item.title: Saskatchewan workers adapt to extreme heat as heat warnings cover the province
item.description: Construction and lawn care workers in Saskatchewan are adjusting their routines as an intense heat wave brings widespread heat warnings across the province. In Saskatoon and other communities, workers...

item.title: Highlights from the June 2026 Edition of Report on Business Magazine
item.description: The June 2026 edition of Report on Business magazine features a diverse set of business stories and profiles that reflect current trends in Canadian commerce and leadership. The issue covers Lululemon...

item.title: Saskatoon riverboat halts cruises amid low river levels and questions water management decisions
item.description: The Prairie Lily riverboat in Saskatoon has suspended its cruise operations due to unusually low water levels in the South Saskatchewan River, following a reduction in water flows from Lake Diefenbake...

item.title: Essendon considers James Hird coaching return amid ongoing club debate
item.description: A renewed push is underway within influential circles connected to the Essendon Football Club to consider the return of former premiership player James Hird as senior coach, highlighting the club’s on...

item.title: Burnaby Hockey Player Overcomes Paralysis to Graduate High School
item.description: A Burnaby high school student and hockey player, who was paralyzed in an accident last year, will be able to walk across the stage for his graduation. The accident, which had a profound impact on his ...

item.title: Congress to Deliberate Karnataka Cabinet Formation Following Leadership Transition
item.description: The Congress party leadership is holding consultations in New Delhi regarding the structure of the Karnataka government following the recent leadership transition. Siddaramaiah, 77, who resigned as th...

item.title: DOE sees likely fuel price rollback next week amid easing global oil pressures
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has indicated that there is a strong possibility of a rollback in domestic petroleum product prices next week, driven by easing tensions in global oil markets. According...

item.title: Labor budget seeks to rebalance wages and investment income amid concerns over modern employment conditions
item.description: The article is an opinion piece examining Australia’s contemporary labour market and the Albanese Labor government’s budget response, particularly under Treasurer Jim Chalmers. It argues that modern e...
